speakers.role: Managing Director & Head of Data and AI

sessions.body: Most companies have shipped AI by now: copilots, dashboards, recommendation engines. The models work, the data platforms exist, yet AI still usually sits behind a human. It advises, but rarely acts. That gap comes down to orchestration, governance and organization design. This session will explore what must be in place before AI can run autonomously, and what changes when it does. We’ll walk through enterprise scenarios where agentic AI runs on a Databricks backbone, and the architecture and governance choices that made it possible. We’ll also cover where human oversight still belongs and…

sessions.title: The Agentic Lakehouse: Optiver’s AI Data Stack
sessions.body: This session deep-dives into how we’ve integrated AI into the core of our data workflows.We will showcase three key innovations: Custom Databricks MCP: How to deploy a centralized MCP deployment enabling LLMs to interact seamlessly with lakehouse resources while maintaining strict context management. Self-healing dashboards: A “prompt-to-production” workflow that automates dashboard deployment and utilizes Playwright-based agents to autonomously detect and fix UI or deployment errors. Agentic research: Moving from static answers to reproducible insights. We’ll demonstrate agents that conduct…

sessions.title: The Alpha Advantage: Building the Intelligent Capital Markets Firm with Databricks

speakers.name: Adam Towne
speakers.role: Director of Product, Scaled Analytics & Funds

sessions.body: Markets look calm, but underneath, there is a rapid reshuffling of outcomes driven by technology, capital intensity, and AI. In this environment, the edge isn’t models— it’s proprietary data and the ability to operationalize it.With Databricks, firms like RBC Capital Markets, Ares Management, and LSEG are automating workflows, scaling AI, and turning data into a durable competitive advantage.

sessions.title: The Data Gap in Women's Football & How FIFPro and Databricks Are Helping to Close It
sessions.body: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off in the USA days before this summit, billions will follow the men's game, but women footballers still fight for visibility and basic protections. FIFPro, representing 65,000+ professional footballers, is using data to change that.For over a decade, FIFPro has surveyed women footballers worldwide on working conditions, welfare and employment rights. But that data lived on laptops in fragmented spreadsheets with critical insights locked away.In this session, Blend shares how we built a data platform on Databricks to power evidence-based advocacy. We'll cover…

sessions.title: The Databridge Platform: Connecting Data Silos Seamlessly With Databricks
sessions.body: We introduce the Databridge Platform, a solution developed by Rakuten Group, Inc. to address the significant data management challenges inherent in their vast global ecosystem. Rakuten faces issues such as data and infrastructure silos, complex data access procedures and data duplication.Built on the Databricks ecosystem, the Databridge Platform leverages technologies like Unity Catalog, Data Access Management Service (DAMS) and AI/BI Genie. Its architecture prioritizes a single workspace for ease of setup while retaining flexibility for dedicated tenant workspaces. The platform's strategic…

sessions.title: The Full Databricks App Experience
sessions.body: What does a real Databricks App look like in production? In this session, we walk through an internal tool we use every day: an agentic Lakeflow generation system built entirely on the Databricks platform. Starting from raw Unity Catalog tables, the app uses foundational models orchestrated in a LangGraph loop to profile data, design pipelines, validate transformations, and generate fully deployable Lakeflow artefacts from Silver through to Gold.We’ll show how application state, specifications, and user preferences are managed in Lakebase, enabling a seamless, iterative build experience…

sessions.title: The Intelligent Healthcare System: How Agentic AI is Redefining Care Delivery and Coverage
sessions.body: The next wave of healthcare transformation won't be driven by data alone — it will be driven by AI that can reason, act and operate across the full continuum of care. In this session, we explore how leading health systems and health plans are moving beyond dashboards and static models to deploy agentic AI workflows built on Databricks — where Genie enables clinical and operational teams to query complex data in plain language, and Mosaic AI orchestrates multi-step agents that surface insights at the point of decision. From reducing prior authorization friction to accelerating discharge…

sessions.title: The Lakehouse Behind a Modern CDP
sessions.body: Building a CDP isn’t just a UI and APIs—it’s a data platform problem at scale. This talk shows how Warner Bros. Discovery built the Databricks-powered “back of house” for Mosaic, supporting audience segmentation and activation across millions of subscribers. We’ll walk through the data plane that converts high-volume first-party events (client SDK + server events) into governed Delta tables using Spark Structured Streaming, then runs incremental batch pipelines to produce custom attributes and curated event views for segmentation across identity grains. We’ll also cover the audience…

sessions.title: The Open Handshake: Scaling Multi-Cloud Delivery via Delta Sharing and Iceberg
sessions.body: Client demand is shifting. They don’t want our data; they want it in their platform. We solved the “Vendor Lock-in” crisis by building an Open Data Passport. This session deconstructs how we leveraged Delta Sharing and Apache Iceberg UniForm to deliver high-scale healthcare products to clients without moving a single byte of raw data. We’ll share the engineering “scars” of managing cross-cloud permissions and how we turned an interoperability headache into a competitive product advantage for our global ecosystem. Demo: A live cross-platform “handshake” between a Databricks source and a…

sessions.title: The Talent Engineering Playbook : How Yanolja Built a High-Performance Data Culture
sessions.body: A modern data platform is just the foundation; success depends on the teams steering it. For Yanolja, Korea’s leading travel-tech unicorn, scaling innovation required a systematic talent strategy to match rapid technical growth.This session offers an inside look at enterprise-scale talent evolution. We deconstruct an "assess-and-architect" methodology, using data-driven diagnostics to bridge skill gaps. A key pillar was establishing an enterprise-wide data capability framework. This created a shared language for skills and maturity — acting as a cultural catalyst that aligns leadership,…

sessions.title: The Write Way: Building Bi-Directional Data Pipelines in the Lakehouse
sessions.body: Ever wondered how to bridge the gap between analytical data and operational workflows without breaking your lakehouse architecture? What if business users could edit data directly in a web app while maintaining a single source of truth? Traditional lakehouses excel at analytics but struggle with user-driven updates, often creating silos or complex workarounds.There’s a better way—in this session we’ll cover a full writeback loop where lakehouse (Unity Catalog) syncs to PostgreSQL (Lakebase) for operational speed, a Databricks app offers an intuitive editing interface and Lakeflow jobs sync…

sessions.title: Transforming Governance Into a Scalable Data & AI Capability in the Data Mesh Era
sessions.body: As enterprises scale AI and data products across domains, traditional governance models fail to keep pace. Historical approaches to data ownership no longer flex to meet data product demands. Domain autonomy creates inconsistency. AI amplifies risk. This presentation presents a scalable operating model that integrates data literacy, enterprise governance, governance engineering and domain enablement into a single cohesive approach built on the modern data platform. Using a federated data mesh architecture powered by Databricks, this modern approach to data ownership transforms governance from…

sessions.title: Translating Nubank’s Data Lake Access Control Model Into Databricks Unity Catalog
sessions.body: Nubank implemented consistent, fine-grained access control across its multi-platform data environment by translating its internal data lake access control model into Databricks Unity Catalog permissions. This model uses centrally managed "Permission Groups" (based on geography, data domains and column sensitivity) to govern access to over 300K tables for 5K+ users, enforcing control at the table and column level. The presentation will detail the architectural implementation, the programmatic translation process using Unity Catalog APIs, and the application of read-time masking for sensitive…

sessions.title: Travel & Hospitality: The Making of the Modern Hospitality Experience— Humans, Data, and the Age of Anticipation
sessions.body: The best hospitality has always been about anticipation— knowing what a guest needs before they ask. AI is now making that possible at scale, but only when it's built on unified, governed data. With customer information still fragmented across legacy systems and siloed tools, most brands can't recognize the same traveler across channels, let alone act on real-time context. The brands pulling ahead are the ones turning data into a moat and AI into a co-pilot for every guest-facing and operational moment.Hear from leading travel and hospitality brands on how they're doing exactly that with…

sessions.title: Turning Data Requests Into Self-Service: Building an AI-Powered Analytics Product Users Love
sessions.body: Ticketmaster's operations team was drowning in data requests. Every "how many fans showed up to buy tickets last week?" question cost engineering hours. We were spending over 8 weeks/year being human query engines. We needed to give users the power to answer their own questions.We built OMT Wizard: a conversational analytics tool that lets non-technical users ask questions in plain English and get instant answers. Powered by Databricks Genie, it launched to 500+ users and achieved high stickiness. The users who try it keep coming back. This session shares product lessons from building…

sessions.title: Underwriting the Future: How Leading Insurers Turn Proprietary Data into Competitive Advantage with Databricks
sessions.body: Insurers today are caught between rising claims complexity, evolving risks, and policyholders who expect more— faster. The carriers pulling ahead aren't just building better models. They're winning on data: who owns it, who can access it, and how quickly they can act on it.In this session, you'll see how leading insurers use Databricks to break down data silos, operationalize AI at scale, and put natural language insights directly in the hands of underwriters, claims teams, and distributors— turning data strategy into smarter risk selection, faster settlements, and profitable growth that…

sessions.title: Unified Governance for Enterprise Analytics and AI
sessions.body: We implemented a unified governance architecture for enterprise analytics leveraging Databricks Unity Catalog as the authoritative control plane. The platform designed with a configuration-driven, dynamic attribute-based access control (ABAC) model with fully enforced row-level security (RLS) and column-level security (CLS) ensuring precise, policy-driven data access across the organization — including hierarchical visibility for cost owners and business leaders aligned to their reporting structures. This design prevents governance drift, reduces operational complexity, centralizes policy…

sessions.title: Unifying Upstream Intelligence with Generative AI
sessions.body: PetroWizard & IntelliSense utilizes an agent-based generative AI architecture on Databricks to address PETRONAS's challenge of fragmented, largely unstructured upstream data (90% unstructured, across OSDU, PI, SQL, etc.). Engineers previously spent 3–5 days locating data. This solution, featuring a Central Orchestrator and four specialized agents (Subsurface, Surface, Sensor, Model Router), enables cross-domain reasoning and model-agnostic, governed data intelligence. Outcomes include reducing data discovery time to 30–45 seconds, achieving 90%+ query accuracy, high user satisfaction (4/5…

sessions.title: Unity Catalog Implementation at Navy Federal Credit Union
sessions.body: We will summarize our successful integration of Unity Catalog (UC) at Navy Federal Credit Union (NFCU), resulting in $10M savings. Our initiative established UC as the central access point for data assets within the Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) and redesigned the enterprise ETL framework.Key outcomes include: Accelerated development cycles: Reduced pipeline build and deployment time by 40%, enabling faster time-to-market for data products. Performance optimization: Achieved a 50% reduction in overall runtime and a 40% decrease in operational costs through efficient resource utilization and…

sessions.title: Unlocking Antibody Space With Databricks Vector Search
sessions.body: Antibodies form the bedrock of human health, and the body’s ability to create what is estimated to be a quintillion unique variants creates an impressive scaling issue for their study. Molecules derived from antibodies are now the fastest growing therapeutic category, and our understanding of how to design and modify antibodies requires solutions that scale. Databricks Vector Search provides an engineered solution for scaling up similarity search to 1 billion embeddings per endpoint, and by embedding antibody sequences with the protein language model AMPLIFY we will describe how this system…

sessions.title: Unlocking Cross-Engine Governance With Scan APIs
sessions.body: While companies have embraced the data lakehouse architecture and benefits it provides, maintaining data governance has become a much more complicated story. Many companies today implement cumbersome workarounds, such as processing and maintaining multiple copies of datasets, duplicating access policies in different systems, or worse bypassing access controls altogether. In this talk, we'll explore some of the current problems with data governance and the data lakehouse architecture and how Scan APIs can address some of those challenges.

sessions.title: Using AI to Migrate Semantic Models and Dashboards to Databricks Metric Views and AI/BI
sessions.body: At HP, we are working to integrate more of our business intelligence and analytics directly into the Databricks platform. To minimize impacts on analytics teams and their partners across organizations, we needed a way to automate and accelerate migrations of legacy semantic models and dashboards into Databricks. We developed a scalable AI based approach to parse existing BI artifacts, translate semantic models into Metric Views, convert existing visualizations into Databricks Dashboards and validate the results. This presentation shares lessons learned across multiple migrations, including…

sessions.title: Using Databricks Geospatial Processing at Scale
sessions.body: Our team started on Geospatial journey with Databricks DBR 16.X, from legacy project migration and continued to expand by ingesting and processing three different geospatial datasets (HD USHR maps, public OSM and curated OSM (Overture)). We applied different optimization techniques to make spatial joins more performant and worked closely with Databricks Geospatial team in testing out incremental improvements in DBR, culminating in large scale performance increase on DBR 17.X bringing support to geospatial data types (GEOMETRY, GEOGRAPHY) as dedicated data types that could be stored in delta…

sessions.title: Utilities: Modernizing the Grid with AI and Real-Time Data
sessions.body: Discover how forward-thinking utilities are reimagining grid management, customer experience, and renewable integration through advanced analytics and AI. EDP and Eversource discuss how they are using the Databricks to turn complex operational data into actionable intelligence by improving reliability, reducing costs, and driving the clean energy transition at scale.

sessions.title: Virtual Delta Tables: Building a Multimodal Lakehouse
sessions.body: Managing multimodal inference is one of the defining challenges facing AI teams. LLMs now consider documents, images, and videos, producing higher quality results and reducing time wasted with data prep. The lakehouse architecture solves part of the problem but many teams are struggling to incorporate an increasing variety of data into their workflow. Rather than ingesting, re-encoding, or modifying massive troves of existing data, multimodal teams are looking to work with original artifacts.This talk introduces Virtual Delta Tables and the associated open source code designed for multimodal…

sessions.title: Weaving Data Into the Financial Fabric: How Nasdaq Uses Databricks to Power the Innovation Economy
sessions.body: Nasdaq is the trusted fabric of the world’s financial system, and data is at the heart of everything we do. Learn how we’re using Databricks to drive insights, support decision-making and deliver industry-leading financial solutions. Using Delta Lake’s open formats and Unity Catalog, we’ve consolidated disparate data stores into a unified corporate data lakehouse to create a single source of truth for enterprise data. We’ll demo how we’re using AgentBricks and Databricks Apps to democratize access to data while maintaining oversight and governance.You’ll also discover how our Capital Access…

sessions.title: What Worked, What Didn’t: Virgin Atlantic’s Flight to Trusted Generative BI
sessions.body: As decision-making at Virgin Atlantic sped up, our BI stack creaked. Leaders wanted answers in hours and questions increasingly cut across commercial, operational, and customer data. Like many teams, we turned to GenBI to close that gap. Our first attempt didn’t go well. Answers arrived faster, but confidence dropped. Without shared metric definitions, visible lineage, or governance designed in from the start, ambiguity scaled faster than trust. “Is this right?” became the default question again. In this session, we share how we rebuilt GenBI on trusted foundations using Databricks. We’ll…

sessions.title: What’s New in Apache Spark™ 4.1?
sessions.body: Explore the exciting new features of Apache Spark 4.1 with major new capabilities for both data engineering and analytics workflows. We introduce Spark Declarative Pipelines (SDP) to let users define what their data pipelines should accomplish while Spark orchestrates execution, dependencies, parallelism and retries, an official Real-Time Mode in Structured Streaming for sub-second, continuous low-latency processing, and extensive improvements to the PySpark ecosystem with Arrow-native UDFs/UDTFs, Python Data Source filter pushdown, and better Python worker logging. The release also brings…

sessions.title: When Redis Isn’t the Answer: Serving Lakehouse Data at Scale
sessions.body: As our feature and operational use cases grew, maintaining separate Redis/DynamoDB stacks alongside the data lake added latency, skew and ops burden. At Superhuman (formerly Grammarly), we adopted Lakebase to serve low‑latency data directly from Databricks, simplifying our architecture while meeting strict SLAs. This session details our setup and results: how we modelled entities and keys, tuned Lakebase capacity and read replicas for high QPS and enforced freshness with Change Data Feed–driven syncs. We’ll show code and configs for provisioning online stores, publishing tables, and querying…

sessions.title: Why Caresource Chose Databricks AI/BI Over Traditional BI for Regulatory Reporting
sessions.body: CareSource initially planned to migrate hundreds of state-mandated SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) reports to Power BI Paginated Reports. However, with a mature Databricks platform already in place, that approach would have required added data movement, duplicated governance logic, and new licensing costs. CareSource and West Monroe share how a Databricks AI/BI pilot reshaped the strategy. By keeping regulatory reporting in Databricks, CareSource eliminated downstream exports, improved reliability, and reduced runtimes from hours to minutes. We’ll review the scalable architecture,…

sessions.title: Why It's Time to Take AI/BI Seriously
sessions.body: I'm a Power BI developer by trade, and for years I've refused to engage with other data visualization platforms. Every time I did, I just found it was lacking capabilities or had serious deficiencies compared to Power BI. Then 2025 hit and Databricks invested heavily in improving AI/BI, metric views and Genie. Now, AI/BI is ready to be a legitimate challenger in the Data Visualisation space.In this talk I will show you how you can use AI/BI, metric views and Genie to get the most out of your data. We will start from basics, building a metric view, using it to build a dashboard and then…

sessions.title: Why Vector Stores Are Not Enough: Using Lakebase as a Durable Memory Layer for Autonomous Agents
sessions.body: We are told that Vector Databases are the 'Memory' for AI Agents. But they give you a probabilistic similarity score. In enterprise workflows, 'probably' isn't good enough. Agents need deterministic, transactional state. We will use Lakebase as a memory layer for agents, enabling them to remember actions, lock resources, and handle concurrency. We will build a 'Stateful Worker' agent live with: The 'Two-Phase Commit' Prompt: Forcing an LLM to lock a row in Lakebase before calling an external API, preventing hallucinated actions. Time Travel for Agents: Leveraging Lakebase Branching to create…

sessions.title: Your AI Strategy is Only as Good as Your People Strategy
sessions.body: As AI moves from experimentation to enterprise impact, success depends on workforce readiness, not just technology. Market leaders have upskilled over 50% of their employees and are redesigning workflows around AI, while 60% of organizations still struggle to generate value from their investments. In this session, Robin Sutara and Melanie Botha explore how to build an AI-enabled workforce by aligning culture, people, process and enablement with your data strategy. You'll learn how to assess your data and AI maturity, close critical skills gaps at scale through structured learning programs,…
